title: General aspects of internal noise in spiking neural networks
summary: This study examines the impact of additive and multiplicative noise on both a single leaky integrate-and-fire (LIF) neuron and a trained spiking neural network (SNN). Noise was introduced at different stages of neural processing, including the input current, membrane potential, and output spike generation. The results show that multiplicative noise applied to the membrane potential has the most detrimental effect on network performance, leading to a significant degradation in accuracy. This is primarily due to its tendency to suppress membrane potentials toward large negative values, effectively silencing neuronal activity. To address this issue, input pre-filtering strategies were evaluated, with a sigmoid-based filter demonstrating the best performance by shifting inputs to a strictly positive range. Under these conditions, additive noise in the input current becomes the dominant source of performance degradation, while other noise configurations reduce accuracy by no more than 1\%, even at high noise intensity. Additionally, the study compares the effects of common and uncommon noise across neuron populations in hidden layer, revealing that SNNs exhibit greater robustness to common noise. Overall, the findings identify the most critical noise mechanisms affecting SNNs and provide practical approaches for improving their robustness.

AI.tldr: 本研究系统研究了加性和乘性噪声对泄漏积分发放神经元和训练好的脉冲神经网络的影响，发现膜电位乘性噪声对网络性能损害最大，并提出了基于Sigmoid的输入预滤波策略来提升鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 脉冲神经网络在实际硬件部署中面临各种噪声干扰，但不同噪声类型和注入位置对SNN性能的影响尚未得到系统研究，需要识别最关键的噪声机制并开发有效的鲁棒性提升方法。
AI.method: 研究在泄漏积分发放神经元和训练好的SNN中，在输入电流、膜电位和输出脉冲生成等不同处理阶段分别引入加性和乘性噪声，并评估了包括Sigmoid滤波器在内的输入预滤波策略，同时比较了神经元群体间常见噪声与不常见噪声的影响。
AI.result: 膜电位乘性噪声对网络性能损害最严重，导致准确率显著下降，主要原因是它将膜电位抑制到大的负值从而沉默神经元活动；Sigmoid预滤波将输入移至严格正范围后，输入电流加性噪声成为主要性能退化源，其他噪声配置在高强度下准确率下降不超过1%；SNN对常见噪声表现出更强的鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 研究识别了影响SNN的最关键噪声机制，特别是膜电位乘性噪声的危害性，提出的Sigmoid输入预滤波策略能有效提升系统鲁棒性，为实际硬件部署中的噪声缓解提供了实用方法，并揭示了SNN对常见噪声的内在鲁棒性特征。

title: Modeling of Self-sustained Neuron Population without External Stimulus
summary: Self-sustained neural activity in the absence of ongoing external input is a fundamental feature of nervous system dynamics, yet the conditions under which it can emerge in biophysically grounded network models remain incompletely understood. We studied whether a recurrent network of Hodgkin-Huxley neurons with spike-timing-dependent plasticity and intrinsic stochasticity can maintain autonomous activity after brief transient stimulation. The simulated network comprised 200 neurons (160 excitatory, 40 inhibitory) with 80% connection probability, incorporating excitatory and inhibitory STDP, probabilistic vesicle release, probabilistic synapse formation, receptor variability, and voltage-dependent inhibition. After a brief 200 ms initialization stimulus to 30 excitatory neurons, the network received no further external input. In one 1800 s simulation and two additional 500 s simulations, the network maintained sparse, irregular activity without ongoing drive. In the 1800 s run, 67% of neurons exhibited mean firing rates below 1 Hz, the population mean firing rate was 1.13 +/- 1.34 Hz, participation increased across longer observation windows, and population-mean Fano factors remained near 1-2, consistent with irregular spike timing. Raster activity also showed spontaneous qualitative reorganizations in collective firing patterns over time. These findings suggest that recurrent Hodgkin-Huxley networks with plastic and stochastic synapses can sustain long-duration autonomous activity in a sparse firing regime after brief initialization.

AI.tldr: 该研究通过模拟包含200个霍奇金-赫胥黎神经元、具有尖峰时序依赖可塑性和内在随机性的循环网络，证明了生物物理基础网络模型在短暂瞬态刺激后能够维持长期自主活动。研究发现这种网络可以在稀疏放电状态下维持不规则的自发活动，为理解神经系统自主活动机制提供了计算模型支持。
AI.motivation: 神经系统在没有持续外部输入的情况下维持自主活动是其基本动力学特征，但在生物物理基础网络模型中这种活动产生的条件尚未完全明确。本研究旨在探索具有尖峰时序依赖可塑性和内在随机性的循环霍奇金-赫胥黎神经元网络是否能够在短暂瞬态刺激后维持自主活动，填补了生物物理模型自主活动机制理解上的空白。
AI.method: 研究构建了一个包含200个神经元（160个兴奋性，40个抑制性）的循环网络模型，连接概率为80%，整合了兴奋性和抑制性STDP、概率性囊泡释放、概率性突触形成、受体变异性和电压依赖性抑制等生物物理机制。网络在200毫秒的初始化刺激后不再接收任何外部输入，通过1800秒和两个500秒的模拟来观察自主活动的维持情况。
AI.result: 模拟结果显示网络能够在没有持续驱动的情况下维持稀疏、不规则的活动模式。在1800秒的模拟中，67%的神经元平均放电频率低于1赫兹，群体平均放电频率为1.13±1.34赫兹，参与度随观察窗口延长而增加，群体平均Fano因子保持在1-2之间，符合不规则尖峰时序特征。活动轨迹还显示出集体放电模式随时间发生自发的质变重组。
AI.conclusion: 研究表明具有可塑性和随机性突触的循环霍奇金-赫胥黎网络能够在稀疏放电状态下维持长期自主活动，这为理解神经系统自主活动的生物物理机制提供了重要计算证据。这些发现表明生物物理细节和突触可塑性在维持自主神经活动中起着关键作用，为未来研究神经系统动力学和计算功能提供了新的模型框架。
